extraction.article_title: Smart Slider updates hijacked to push malicious WordPress, Joomla versions

ttps.description: Exploit public-facing web application by abusing a compromised plugin update channel to implant backdoors in WordPress/Joomla deployments.
Server software component: malicious functionality embedded into a legitimate CMS plugin update to persist on the target system.
Unix shell command execution through web-request-controlled inputs, including crafted HTTP headers.
PHP execution via authenticated backdoor using eval-style code execution.
Create a hidden administrative user in a WordPress/Joomla environment to maintain privileged access.

attack_chain: Distribute trojanized Smart Slider Pro update
Plant multiple backdoors in plugin files
Exfiltrate sensitive data and enable remote command execution

proposed_vectors.name_en: Trojanized CMS plugin update / supply-chain implant
proposed_vectors.name_ko: 악성 CMS 플러그인 업데이트 / 공급망 임플란트
헤더 기반 비인증 명령 실행 백도어

proposed_vectors.name_en: Authenticated PHP eval / OS command execution backdoor
proposed_vectors.name_ko: 인증 기반 PHP eval / OS 명령 실행 백도어

proposed_phase_updates.update: Add detection for malicious plugin-update implants that preserve normal functionality while injecting backdoors into CMS main files.
Add HTTP-header-triggered execution checks for WordPress/Joomla plugin callbacks and admin-ajax/admin endpoints.

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A hidden backdoor reads attacker-controlled values from HTTP headers and passes them to an OS command execution sink. If the application concatenates header content into a shell command, an attacker can trigger command execution without authentication.
detect: Out-of-band file creation or command execution evidence on the server
Unexpected process spawning from the web server user
HTTP 200/500 paired with a new file containing uid/gid output

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: An authenticated backdoor accepts a parameter and passes it directly to a shell command. Command separators can be used to append arbitrary commands if the input is not sanitized.
detect: Presence of /tmp/wp_cmdi_poc.txt or equivalent server-side artifact
Delayed response consistent with command execution
Server logs showing the injected parameter reaching a command handler

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A malicious plugin update can introduce PHP code execution through eval, assert, or dynamic include on attacker-controlled input. Supplying PHP code as data can result in direct server-side execution.
detect: Unexpected output marker 'done' in HTTP response
WAF or application logs showing PHP tags in request parameters

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A backdoor or implant may expose privileged functions without proper session validation. Attackers can replay a crafted request to reach admin-only code paths if the plugin trusts a header, cookie, or static token.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload:
X-Plugin-Auth: admin
X-Request-Action: create_user
X-Request-Role: administrator
detect: Unexpected 200/302 from endpoints that should require authentication
Creation of an administrator user without a valid login session
Audit logs showing admin actions from unauthenticated requests

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: If the malicious or vulnerable plugin exposes object identifiers for users, posts, or configuration items, changing the numeric ID can access or modify another tenant's data without authorization.
detect: Disclosure of another user's profile or metadata
Same response content for multiple guessed IDs
Authorization checks missing in server-side code paths

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A hidden administrative user can be created by abusing an installer or maintenance endpoint that lacks CSRF or session checks. If the endpoint trusts form fields directly, attacker-supplied role values can produce a privileged account.
detect: New administrator user appears in the CMS user list
Database record for a user created by an unexpected source IP or no session
Role set to administrator without a valid admin workflow
